intent: Iterate on whisper-writer voice-to-text config and code by repeatedly killing the running app and restarting it to test model/compute-type changes.
finding: Across three whisper-writer sessions (4032d09c, 57c147f5, 4fb269aa) the same manual loop recurs: pgrep for the process, kill it, wait for GPU to free, start it again in the background, tail the log. In 4032d09c alone this cycle ran ~15 times in one session while swapping between large-v3-turbo / distil-small.en / distil-medium.en / small / medium and adjusting int8/float16. The loop also interacts with a fcntl lock on /tmp/whisper-writer.lock and systemd user-service restarts. Outcome across cycles is identical (process replaced, log tailed); a single durable artifact — e.g. a `dev-restart.sh` or `make restart` wrapping pid cleanup, lock-file removal, background start, and log tail, or a systemd user unit invoked via `systemctl --user restart whisper-writer` — would replace the manual sequence.

intent: Install a chosen subset of skills from the everything-claude-code repo into ~/.claude/skills/ as symlinks, resolving any dependency skills the chosen ones require.
finding: In session bda7a17f the user listed ~11 skill names (skill-stocktake, context-budget, strategic-compact, skill-comply, seo, security-scan, research-ops + deps, database-migrations, content-engine/brand-voice, codebase-onboarding, accessibility) and asked for symlinks, which expanded to 17 links after dependency resolution. Single session, single instance this window, but the shape is a repeatable list-driven install. A `link-ecc-skills <name...>` script that resolves declared skill dependencies and creates the symlinks would replace the manual batch.

intent: Rename a forked local project and rewire its git origin to a freshly created GitHub repo under my own account.
finding: In session 4fb269aa the user had me create the GitHub repo jonathanmoregard/voxtype and update the local remote. One instance this window; no cross-session recurrence observed. Flagged low as a watch item — if this pattern reappears, a `gh-rehome <new-name>` helper (create repo, set remote, initial push) would collapse the steps.
